content: "Operation Sindoor was not an end, and it was just the beginning," Lt Gen Rajiv Ghai, who played a key role in executing the operation in his capacity as the Army's Director General of Military Operations (DGMO), said.

content: The exact cause of the death will be known once the forensic science lab submits its report, police said

content: The oath-taking ceremony was attended by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, Home Minister Amit Shah, JD(U) chief Nitish Kumar and other senior leaders of the ruling NDA in the State

content: Joseph says ‘the issue of ministerial representation’ for the party will be settled amicably through discussions

title: Naidu directs officials to take result-oriented approach, says A.P. inherited ₹9.74 lakh crore in debt
content: Addressing the 7th Collectors’ Conference, the Chief Minister said the coalition government had inherited pending bills worth ₹1.30 lakh crore and a diversion of ₹10,000 crore from 94 centrally sponsored schemes

title: Kerala polls: Goat head display during Thavanur victory procession triggers police case, political outrage
content: Two IUML workers booked for publicly displaying severed goat’s head during victory celebrations after defeat of LDF candidate K.T. Jaleel in Thavanur in Malappuram

title: Requisite majority support to form Tamil Nadu government not established: Governor Arlekar to Vijay
content: Unlike his meeting with the Governor on Wednesday when Mr. Vijay was accompanied only by six newly-elected MLA-designates, on Thursday, a few individuals, who are advising the TVK founder on political and legal matters also accompanied Mr. Vijay to the Lok Bhavan.

title: Watch: Can Mamata Banerjee refuse to resign? What the Constitution says
content:
A day after a crushing defeat in the West Bengal Assembly elections, Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has refused to step down, alleging that nearly 100 seats were “stolen”. 

But can a Chief Minister actually refuse to resign after losing an election? Here’s what the Constitution says.

title: RPF detects theft of railway signal cable in Mysuru, stolen property recovered
content: At the time of the incident, the accused were engaged as daily wage labourers for a water pipeline project and had observed exposed railway cables during excavation work

title: Census 2027 data fully protected under Census Act, 1948, says A.P. Director of Census Operations
content: Andhra Pradesh Director of Census Operations and Director of Citizen Registrations, J. Nivas, says individual data cannot be shared under the RTI Act, used as evidence in courts, or disclosed to any institution

title: DMK’s allies urge T.N. Governor to invite TVK chief Vijay to form government
content: “The BJP is using the Governor and taking a stand against the Constitution. That is why the Governor is delaying the opportunity for Mr. Vijay to form the government,” CPI(M) State secretary P. Shanmugam alleged

title: ‘Police heading in right direction in Rath murder probe,’ says Suvendu Adhikari
content: Chandranath Rath was shot dead by bike-borne assailants who intercepted his vehicle near Doltala in Madhyamgram around 10.30 p.m. on May 6, forced it to stop and opened fire at point-blank range before fleeing, according to police

title: Vizianagaram administration races to wrap up land acquisition for major projects, including tribal university
content: The State government has asked senior officials to complete the acquisition of about 800 acres within two months for projects including Tarakarama Teertha Sagar, Thotapalli, Uttarandhra Sujala Sravanthi and the Central Tribal University of Andhra Pradesh

title: Sentinels of Simari: One year after Sindoor, villagers try to reclaim narrative of normalcy
content: It is from this frontier village surrounded by high mountains that the Army fired shells across the Krishan Ganga river to signal the start of Operation Sindoor

title: HC asks TTD to decide on darshan quota for autoimmune, rare disease patients in 4 months
content: The petitioner has sought the inclusion of severe autoimmune disorders, rare diseases and serious haematological conditions in the TTD’s special darshan quota for medical cases; the High Court passed the order on April 28

title: Questioning religious practices will break religion and civilisation: Supreme Court in Dawoodi Bohras case
content: The nine-judge Constitution Bench is hearing petitions related to discrimination against women at religious places, including the Sabarimala temple in Kerala, and on the ambit and scope of the religious freedom practised by multiple faiths, including Dawoodi Bohras.

title: Demographic imbalance in India needs more attention than population control, says RSS chief Mohan Bhagwat
content: With to Common Civil Code, Mr. Bhagwat referred to the introduction of the law in a few States in India, including Uttarakhand, and expressed optimism that the Uniform Civil Code will gradually be implemented across India

title: Sri Lanka’s former President Chandrika Kumaratunga wishes Vijay on ‘remarkable achievement’
content: “I recall with pleasure the long and cordial association between Tamil Nadu and Sri Lanka and look forward to our continued friendly relationship under your governance,” said Sri Lanka’s former President Chandrika Bandaranaike Kumaratunga

title: During Operation Sindoor, India destroyed 13 Pak aircraft, 11 airfields: Air Marshal Bharti
content: Indian forces successfully neutralized 13 Pakistani aircraft and struck 11 airfields during Op Sindoor, with evidence readily available.  Nine terrorist camps were also decimated on May 7.  Pakistan failed to inflict significant damage on Indian infrastructure, highlighting India's decisive response and strategic restraint in defending its sovereignty.

title: 'Faster repatriation of illegals': India rebuts Bangladesh ‘pushback’ claims after BJP’s Bengal win
content: India urged Bangladesh to expedite nationality verification for illegal immigrants, linking it to repatriation efforts. This response follows Bangladesh's concerns about potential "pushback" incidents after the BJP's West Bengal election victory, with Dhaka seeking cooperation for smooth deportations.

title: 'You put me in jail. Now, it's my turn': Messi tour organiser warns TMC
content: “Just because you have lost in the elections, don’t think that you have been punished. I will lodge a defamation case, and if needed, will go to the Supreme Court. I will fight till the end,” he wrote on Facebook stories, as quoted by Sportstar.

title: Microsoft's first-ever voluntary retirement offer for 8,500-plus US employees: Here's what's in buyout package
content: Microsoft has unveiled its first voluntary retirement program, offering eligible US employees a package including five years of healthcare coverage (one fully paid), cash severance up to 39 weeks' pay, and accelerated stock vesting. This initiative aims to reduce headcount ahead of the new fiscal year, following recent layoffs and executive departures.

title: Yuzvendra Chahal vaping on flight? PBKS spinner takes a dig at Riyan Parag controversy
content: A viral clip from Punjab Kings’ travel vlog sparked controversy after Yuzvendra Chahal appeared to vape during a flight to Hyderabad. However, eagle-eyed fans later claimed the spinner may have been holding a spray device and merely mimicking the act. The moment, which was removed from the original vlog, has now fuelled speculation that Chahal was playfully referencing the recent Riyan Parag vaping controversy.

title: Op Sindoor anniversary: MEA says India gave ‘befitting reply’ to Pak-backed terror
content: India marked the first anniversary of Operation Sindoor, a decisive response to Pakistan-sponsored cross-border terrorism following the deadly Pahalgam attack. The Ministry of External Affairs stated the world recognized the attack and India's right to self-defense. The operation successfully targeted terror launchpads, demonstrating India's commitment to combating terrorism.

title: China should not have ... : Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang after share falls to zero in Beijing
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang declared America a priority for advanced chip sales, stating China should not have access to the "latest and greatest" AI technology. However, he urged the US not to abandon the Chinese market entirely, arguing that global sales and increased tax revenues strengthen American economic and national security.

title: Kerala plus one improvement result 2026 release: Check how to download scorecards
content: Kerala Plus One improvement results for 2026 are now available on results.hse.kerala.gov.in. Students who took the improvement exams between March 5 and March 27 can access their scores by entering registration numbers and dates of birth. School authorities can also check results using school codes. The scorecard includes subject-wise marks and other crucial details.

title: 1 year of Op Sindoor: PM, his cabinet team change DP of their social media handles
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi urged citizens to change their social media display pictures to commemorate the first anniversary of 'Operation Sindoor'. This initiative honors the Indian armed forces' valor and decisive response during the operation along the western border. The campaign aims to express respect for the forces' success and national resolve.

title: Anthropic CEO jokingly ‘admits’ problem about $800bn company that many analysts have been saying
content: AI firm Anthropic is experiencing an astonishing 80x revenue and usage surge, primarily driven by its popular Claude Code service. CEO Dario Amodei humorously admitted the rapid growth is 'too hard to handle,' straining resources. To cope, Anthropic is securing massive computing power from SpaceX, highlighting the intense race for AI dominance and the challenges of hyper-growth.

title: 'Sirf hungama karna mera maqsad nahi': Army's pointed message to Pak on Op Sindoor anniversary
content: Operation Sindoor, launched in response to the Pahalgam terror attack, demonstrated India's 'Atmanirbhar' capability as a force multiplier, with over 65% of defense equipment now domestically manufactured. Lt Gen Rajiv Ghai highlighted the operation's success in achieving objectives through a calibrated, decisive response without escalating into prolonged conflict, underscoring multi-agency coordination and intelligence dominance.

title: Simone Biles 'in therapy' as Jonathan Owens eyes NFL return
content: Simone Biles opens up about her mental health struggles following the Tokyo Olympics, reflecting on the pressure that led her to step away from competition. In a recent interview, the Olympic champion shared how the experience changed her perspective on strength and vulnerability. Two years later, Biles continues to focus on her well-being while keeping her future in gymnastics open, including a possible return for the 2028 Olympics in Los Angeles.

title: What happened to Kyle Loftis? Questions grow after 1320 video founder’s sudden death
content: Kyle Loftis, founder of 1320Video, died Tuesday night, according to the platform’s official statement. His cause of death has not been confirmed yet. Loftis helped build one of the biggest motorsports media brands online through street racing and automotive content. Fans, racers, and creators across social media continue sharing tributes and remembering his influence on modern car culture.

title: Fact check: DNA test rumors for Dianna Russini’s sons are false parody claims
content: Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini’s alleged affair rumors have only gone worse in the last few weeks as she had to step down from her successful career, sparking outrage among fans. While Mike Vrabel’s life remains unchanged, all focus is on the children that Dianna Russini and her husband, Kevin Goldschmidt, share together, as the popular coach gets dragged into it.

title: What is the right age for your child's first smartphone? Parenting experts answer
content: Experts advise parents to consider a child's maturity, not just age, before giving them a first phone. Starting with basic devices and gradually introducing smartphones with monitoring tools is recommended. Open communication about online safety and responsible usage is crucial. Parents should also model healthy screen time habits. This approach helps children navigate the digital world safely and responsibly.

title: China sentences former defence ministers to death in sweeping military graft purge
content: China has handed down death sentences with a two-year reprieve to former defence ministers Wei Fenghe and Li Shangfu for corruption. This significant development is part of President Xi Jinping's extensive anti-graft campaign targeting the military and political elite. Both officials were found guilty of graft-related offenses, with Li Shangfu also accused of seeking improper benefits in personnel appointments.

title: Kerala govt formation: Decision on CM face likely by Sunday as AICC observers meet Cong leaders
content: The Congress is set to announce its Kerala chief ministerial pick by Sunday after consultations with newly elected MLAs and senior leaders. The party high command will make the final decision following a resolution authorizing them to choose the CM candidate. Frontrunners include V D Satheesan, K C Venugopal, and Ramesh Chennithala.

title: Middle East tensions: Iran’s response to US peace proposal expected today
content: Iran is reviewing a US proposal, with a response expected soon, potentially via Pakistani mediators. Tehran prioritizes a full war cessation and sanctions relief over nuclear talks, seeking UN Security Council guarantees.

title: Man posing as Rahul Gandhi’s PA arrested for duping Congress leader of Rs 25 lakh
content: Authorities in Dehradun have made a significant arrest, capturing a man from Amritsar who reportedly tricked a Congress functionary into handing over Rs 25 lakh by masquerading as an aide to Rahul Gandhi, promising a high-ranking party role in return. Drawing on insights from MyNeta.

title: ‘Bengal voted out politics of violence’: Chirag Paswan on killing of Adhikari's aide
content: Union minister Chirag Paswan condemned the killing of BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari’s aide, Chandranath Rath, calling it "politics of violence" that West Bengal voters rejected. Paswan alleged TMC goons were responsible and stated the central government will not tolerate such violence, asserting a BJP government will soon be formed in the state.

title: ‘Look at Virat Kohli’: Sanjay questions Rohit and Suryakumar fitness standards
content: Former India batter Sanjay Manjrekar believes Virat Kohli's elite fitness culture sets a benchmark that Rohit Sharma and Suryakumar Yadav may have fallen short of. Manjrekar suggested that a decline in physical conditioning, particularly after 30, has made things harder for Rohit and Suryakumar, impacting their performance, especially in T20 cricket.

title: Gautam Gambhir faces fresh criticism: Ex-Indian cricketer calls him a 'bully'
content: Former India cricketer Atul Wassan has accused new head coach Gautam Gambhir of being a "bully" with an "my way or the highway" approach, stemming from his Delhi cricket days. While Gambhir has achieved white-ball success, India's Test form has declined, raising questions about his confrontational coaching style's suitability.

title: Father can’t be called kidnapper: HC refuses Habeas Corpus in parental custody dispute
content: The Court refused to entertain a habeas corpus petition filed by a mother seeking custody of her two minor children from their father, stressing that any such disputes must ordinarily be resolved under statutory remedies.

title: Operation Sindoor: Ten strategic lessons for India’s military future
content: Every conflict leaves behind more than tactical outcomes; it generates a blueprint for future preparedness. Operation Sindoor, when examined through a structural and doctrinal lens, reveals a decisive shift in how India must think about air power, attrition, industrial capacity, and integrated warfare. Its most enduring lessons lie not in what was achieved, but in what must now be built.

title: ‘Shocked and saddened’: Kohli mourns death of former teammate Amanpreet Gill
content: Virat Kohli and the cricket fraternity mourned the untimely demise of former Punjab cricketer Amanpreet Singh Gill, who passed away at 36. Gill, an ex-India U-19 pacer, represented Punjab and was part of the inaugural IPL season with Kings XI Punjab. Tributes poured in from former teammates and the Punjab Cricket Association.

title: 'Raja Shivaji' BO day 7 [LIVE]: Riteish Deshmukh film crosses Rs. 50 crore
content: 'Raja Shivaji', directed by and starring Riteish Deshmukh as Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj, is on the verge of crossing Rs. 50 crore India net in its first weekm a historic first for Marathi cinema. Released on Maharashtra Day (May 1, 2026), the Rs. 100 crore production set the highest-ever Marathi first Monday (Rs. 5.60 crore) and delivered an extraordinary weekday hold throughout its opening week. The film features Sanjay Dutt, Abhishek Bachchan, Vidya Balan, Genelia Deshmukh and the debut of Rahyl Deshmukh, with Salman Khan's viral surprise cameo as Jiva Mahala, Ajay-Atul's music and Santosh Sivan's cinematography.

title: 'Attempt to tarnish party image': BJP sends notice to Mann over Punjab blast allegations
content: BJP national general secretary Tarun Chugh has sent a legal notice to Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann. The notice demands an apology for Mann's remarks allegedly linking the BJP to recent blast incidents in Punjab. Chugh accuses Mann of tarnishing the BJP's reputation. The notice warns of legal action if Mann fails to comply within seven days.

title: Is representing India worth it? The draining family cost of raising a chess star
content: India’s chess boom is inspiring young talents, but families face huge financial and logistical struggles behind the scenes. Parents spend lakhs on foreign tournaments, visas, travel, and coaching, often without sponsorship or federation support. Many also deal with poor tournament systems in India and scams abroad. Despite rising success stories like Vaishali and Gukesh, chess parents say survival in the sport remains extremely difficult.

title: ‘Did this because BJP came to power’: Suvendu Adhikari aide’s mother seeks life term for killers
content: Chandranath Rath's mother has called for justice following her son's tragic death at the hands of his assailants, demanding life sentences for the perpetrators. She argues that the attack is a direct consequence of the political upheaval tied to BJP's ascent in power.

title: Pat Cummins offered massive 12 million dollars contract deal to stay with...
content: Australian cricket captain Pat Cummins is reportedly set for a significant contract extension. Cricket Australia is offering a deal that could see him earn nearly AUD 4 million annually until 2029. This move aims to secure top stars amidst the growing allure of global franchise leagues.

title: Bihar cabinet expansion: Nishant Kumar among leaders inducted in Samrat Choudhary govt
content: Bihar's cabinet is set for a major expansion. Nishant Kumar, son of Nitish Kumar, is poised to join the ministry. This move follows the NDA's victory in the assembly polls. Senior NDA leaders are expected to attend the ceremony. The expansion aims to strengthen the alliance and accelerate development in Bihar.

title: 'Diversity Is India's Strength': Supreme Court On Sabarimala-Linked Case
content: Justice BV Nagarathna pointed out "what's troubling the court" while hearing arguments by petitioners defending the grounds taken by the landmark Sabarimala verdict of 2018

title: Satellite Pics Show Iran Strikes Damaged 228 Assets Across 15 US Bases
content: Among the sites that were damaged in Iran's missile and drone strikes was a satellite communications site at al-Udeid Air Base in Qatar. Patriot missile defence systems at bases in Bahrain and Kuwait were also hit.

title: Opinion: Opinion | One Year Of Op Sindoor: Why 'Round 2' Will Be A Different Beast Entirely
content: Another Op Sindoor - highly likely - would have to be framed and executed in quite a different milieu.

title: Fearing Failure, 19-Year-Old NEET Aspirant Dies By Suicide In Odisha
content: According to police, he was found hanging from the ceiling of their rented house in Bhadrak town police station area.

title: BJP Sweeps Scheduled Caste, Tribal Belts In Bengal, Assam
content: The BJP swept all 16 reserved seats for Scheduled Tribes in West Bengal and won 51 of the state's 68 Scheduled Caste constituencies, reducing the Trinamool Congress to just 17 reserved seats

title: Inducements Worth Rs 1,445 Crore Seized During 2026 Assembly Elections: Poll Body
content: West Bengal saw the highest increase in the seizures of 68.92 per cent, and Tamil Nadu saw an increase in seizures of 48.40 per cent in 2026 compared to the corresponding figures during the 2021 elections.

title: Man Arrested For Allegedly Filming Woman Bathing In Bengaluru
content: Police said the woman noticed an unidentified individual allegedly recording a video using a mobile phone while she was inside the bathroom.

title: Char Dham Yatra Sees 32 Pilgrim Deaths In 19 Days, Kedarnath Worst Hit
content: Uttarakhand Forest Minister Subodh Uniyal said health advisories were also shared during the registration process for the yatra.

title: BJP Sends Defamation Notice To Bhagwant Mann Over Punjab Blasts' Allegations
content: Bhagwant Mann on Wednesday held the BJP responsible for the two blast incidents, alleging this was how the party was preparing for the upcoming assembly elections in the state.

title: Stock Market Highlights, Sensex Today: Sensex, Nifty Settle Flat Amid Mixed Global Cues
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Asian markets rally on growing optimism that the US and Iran were nearing an agreement.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Is INDIA Bloc Set To Split After Tamil Nadu, Bengal Results? A Look At The Mess
content: Reportedly, Rahul was quick to realise his folly of not siding with Vijay sooner, the day votes were cast across Tamil Nadu. By April 24-25, he had told a close aide, "We made a blunder in Tamil Nadu."

title: 'Tamil Nadu Insulted': DMK, VCK Support Vijay In Stalemate vs Governor
content: Leading the charge in Vijay's favour was his fellow actor-politician, Kamal Haasan. He said that by not inviting Vijay to form the government, the Governor was "disrespecting the mandate".

title: Amazon Rainforest Near "Point Of No Return" At 1.5 Degrees Warming: Study
content: The study finds that at 22-28% deforestation combined with 1.51.9C of warming, the forest could lose its ability to sustain itself across vast areas.

title: Kerala Congress (M) Faces Existential Crisis After Pala Shocker
content: For the second straight Kerala Assembly election, Kerala Congress (M) failed to reclaim Pala, the constituency KM Mani represented for more than five decades.

title: MK Stalin Gives DMK Allies Free Hand Over Vijay Tie-Up Amid Uncertainty
content: The TVK had reached out to the VCK and the Left parties after failing to secure a majority on its own despite emerging as the largest party in Tamil Nadu elections.

title: Life Insurance vs Fixed Deposit: The Winner On Returns May Surprise You
content: Over long horizons, equity exposure and compounding can deliver returns that beat traditional fixed deposits -- especially after tax.

title: Iran Denies Trump's Claim, Rejects Hitting South Korean Ship In Hormuz
content: Trump claims Iran has "taken some shots" at the South Korean ship and urges Seoul to join operations to restore shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.
